title: Vertical Slice Architecture — NXGN Actions
purpose: Document the implemented vertical-slice API structure: endpoint folders own request-specific behavior, feature Shared/ owns reusable feature code and feature-owned EF entities/configurations, Data/ owns shared database infrastructure, and Nxgn.Actions.Contracts owns public API DTOs.

examples.name: Feature Folder Structure Example 1
examples.code:
Each feature is a vertical slice under `Nxgn.Actions.Api/Features/{Area}/{Feature}/`. The feature folder owns the behavior and the feature-owned persistence artifacts for that module.

Current aggregate-root modules use this shape:

```text
Features/Admin/Workflows/
  GetWorkflowGridList/
    GetWorkflowGridListEndpoint.cs        # POST /grid-list, optional aliases
  GetWorkflowById/
    GetWorkflowByIdEndpoint.cs
  SaveWorkflow/
    SaveWorkflowEndpoint.cs               # endpoint, validator, endpoint-specific command
  PublishWorkflow/
    PublishWorkflowEndpoint.cs
  CloneWorkflow/
    CloneWorkflowEndpoint.cs
  DeleteWorkflowDraft/
    DeleteWorkflowDraftEndpoint.cs
  Shared/
    WorkflowEntities.cs                   # feature-owned aggregate entities
    WorkflowEntityConfigurations.cs       # feature-owned EF configurations
    WorkflowQueries.cs                    # extracted only when useful
    Commands/                             # optional reusable commands
    Mappers/                              # optional reusable mappers
```

Use endpoint folders for behavior that belongs to one request. Use `Shared/` for feature-owned types that multiple endpoints in the same feature need. Use `Data/` only for DbContext, migrations, shared infrastructure, and truly cross-feature entities/configuration.

examples.name: Contract Types (Separate Project) Example 1
examples.code:
Public API contracts live in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`, not in the API feature folders.

The current convention is module-level grouping:

```text
Nxgn.Actions.Contracts/
  Admin/
    WorkflowDTOs.cs
    ComplianceDtos.cs
    EscalationDtos.cs
  Deadlines/
    DeadlineDtos.cs
  Shared/
    PagedResponse.cs
    GridRequest.cs
```

Keep related request/response DTOs together while the module is small enough to scan. Split into per-type files only when file size, ownership, or reuse makes the separate file clearer.

Feature implementation may use private/internal request, validator, command, or mapper types inside the API project, but anything serialized across the public API boundary belongs in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`.

examples.name: Management API Feature Inventory Example 1
examples.code:
Features/
├── Actions/                   # Action management (aggregate root)
│   ├── Save/, Get/, GetGridList/, Delete/
│   ├── Evidence/              # Sub-feature: evidence attachments
│   │   └── Upload/, List/, Delete/
│   ├── Consultations/         # Sub-feature: consultation records
│   │   └── Save/, List/, Delete/
│   ├── Deadlines/             # Sub-feature: deadline tracking
│   │   └── Save/, List/
│   └── Shared/ (Commands, Mappers, Queries)
│
├── Admin/
│   ├── Workflows/             # Workflow definition management
│   │   ├── Save/, Get/, GetGridList/, Delete/, Activate/, Deactivate/
│   │   └── Shared/ (Commands, Mappers, Queries)
│   │
│   ├── ComplianceProfiles/    # Compliance profile configuration
│   │   ├── Save/, Get/, GetGridList/, Delete/
│   │   └── Shared/ (Commands, Mappers, Queries)
│   │
│   ├── EscalationRules/       # Escalation rule management
│   │   ├── Save/, Get/, GetGridList/, Delete/
│   │   └── Shared/ (Commands, Mappers, Queries)
│   │
│   └── Languages/             # Language configuration
│       ├── Save/, Get/, GetGridList/
│       └── Shared/ (Commands, Mappers, Queries)
│
└── Shared/                    # Cross-feature infrastructure
    ├── TenantEndpoint.cs          # Base classes (TenantId, UserId, RequestLanguageId, DefaultLanguageId)
    ├── EntityMapperResult.cs      # EntityMapperResult<T> with NewItems/UpdatedItems/DeletedItems
    └── TranslationService.cs      # Language-aware name/description resolution

examples.name: File Placement Decision Tree Example 1
examples.code:
Use this placement rule when adding or reviewing a module:

| Artifact | Default location | Extract when |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Endpoint registration/handler | `{Feature}/{EndpointName}/{EndpointName}Endpoint.cs` | Never outside the owning feature |
| Endpoint-specific request command | Same endpoint folder/file | It is reused, large enough to obscure the endpoint, or easier to test separately |
| Reusable feature command/query/mapper | `{Feature}/Shared/Commands`, `Shared/Queries`, or `Shared/Mappers` | It becomes shared across features, then promote to an explicit shared service |
| Feature-owned EF entity/configuration | `{Feature}/Shared/*Entities.cs`, `{Feature}/Shared/*EntityConfigurations.cs` | It becomes truly cross-feature/shared platform data |
| Shared EF entity/configuration | `Data/Entities`, `Data/Configuration` | It becomes owned by one feature, then move it back under that feature |
| Public API DTO | `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts/{AreaOrModule}/*Dtos.cs` | Split per type/file when the grouped file becomes hard to review |
| Grid-list endpoint | `POST /api/v1/{entities}/grid-list` | Add `/grid` or `/server-grid` only as compatibility aliases |

Do not move feature-owned persistence types into `Data/` just to satisfy a horizontal layer. Do not call another feature's endpoint command directly.

examples.name: Sub-Feature Organization Example 1
examples.code:
Features/Actions/
├── Evidence/                   # Sub-feature
│   └── Upload/                 # Endpoint folder within sub-feature
│       ├── UploadEvidenceEndpoint.cs
│       └── UploadEvidenceValidator.cs
│
├── Consultations/              # Sub-feature
│   ├── Save/
│   ├── List/
│   └── Delete/
│
├── Deadlines/                  # Sub-feature
│   ├── Save/
│   └── List/
│
└── Shared/                     # Commands/Queries for BOTH parent and sub-features
    ├── Commands/
    │   ├── SaveActionCommand.cs
    │   ├── DeleteActionCommand.cs
    │   ├── UploadEvidenceCommand.cs         # Sub-feature command
    │   └── SaveConsultationCommand.cs       # Sub-feature command
    └── Queries/
        ├── GetActionQuery.cs
        ├── GetActionsQuery.cs
        ├── ActionQueryParameters.cs         # Extends PagedQueryParameters<T>
        └── GetActionEvidenceQuery.cs        # Sub-feature query

rules.rule_text: **One endpoint per folder** — `Save/SaveWorkflowEndpoint.cs`, not mixed with other endpoints
**Save/ for upserts** — single endpoint handles both create and update; SaveRequest inherits from the DTO, `Guid.Empty` signals create
Use feature Shared/ for reusable feature code and feature-owned EF entities/configurations; endpoint-specific commands may remain in the endpoint folder/file.
**Feature folders are self-contained** — no imports from other `Features/{OtherEntity}/`
**DI for cross-feature needs** — inject commands/queries from other features via constructor
**Management endpoints extend `TenantEndpoint`** — provides `TenantId`, `UserId`, `RequestLanguageId`, `DefaultLanguageId`
**DI-registered mapper classes** — `{Entity}EntityMapper` and `{Entity}DTOMapper` as scoped services, not static extension methods
**CommandResult<EntityMapperResult<T>> for saves** — save commands wrap `EntityMapperResult<T>` in `CommandResult` for consistent error handling
**CommandResult for deletes/lifecycle** — delete commands return `CommandResult`, lifecycle returns `CommandResult` or `CommandResult<T>`
**QueryParameters builder classes** — `{Entity}QueryParameters` extending `PagedQueryParameters<T>` with fluent API for grid/count queries
**`IDbContextFactory<ActionsDbContext>`** — always use factory, never inject `ActionsDbContext` directly
POST for grid list: use POST /api/v1/{entities}/grid-list as canonical; /grid and /server-grid are compatibility aliases only; do not add /list.
**POST for delete** — `POST /delete` for batch delete with request body
Put public DTOs in Nxgn.Actions.Contracts; module-level DTO files are acceptable while readable; feature-local DTOs must stay private/internal.
**Import from other feature folders** — use DI, not direct references
Put request-specific business orchestration in the endpoint folder; extract commands, mappers, or services when reuse, complexity, or testability justifies it.
**Create separate Create/ and Update/ folders** — use Save/ for the upsert pattern
**Extend `Endpoint<T>` directly for management endpoints** — use `TenantEndpoint` base classes
**Use static extension methods for mappers** — use DI-registered mapper classes (`{Entity}EntityMapper`, `{Entity}DTOMapper`)
**Define SaveRequest as an independent type** — SaveRequest inherits from the DTO
**Use `Guid?` with null for create detection** — use `Guid` with `Guid.Empty`
**Use IQueryable extension methods for filtering** — use `{Entity}QueryParameters` builder classes
**Filter by TenantId in application code** — RLS handles tenant isolation

sections.title: Feature Folder Structure
content: Each feature is a vertical slice under `Nxgn.Actions.Api/Features/{Area}/{Feature}/`. The feature folder owns the behavior and the feature-owned persistence artifacts for that module.
Current aggregate-root modules use this shape:

Use endpoint folders for behavior that belongs to one request. Use `Shared/` for feature-owned types that multiple endpoints in the same feature need. Use `Data/` only for DbContext, migrations, shared infrastructure, and truly cross-feature entities/configuration.

The current convention is module-level grouping:
Keep related request/response DTOs together while the module is small enough to scan. Split into per-type files only when file size, ownership, or reuse makes the separate file clearer.

Feature implementation may use private/internal request, validator, command, or mapper types inside the API project, but anything serialized across the public API boundary belongs in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`.

sections.title: File Placement Decision Tree
content: Use this placement rule when adding or reviewing a module:
| Endpoint registration/handler | `{Feature}/{EndpointName}/{EndpointName}Endpoint.cs` | Never outside the owning feature |
| Endpoint-specific request command | Same endpoint folder/file | It is reused, large enough to obscure the endpoint, or easier to test separately |
| Reusable feature command/query/mapper | `{Feature}/Shared/Commands`, `Shared/Queries`, or `Shared/Mappers` | It becomes shared across features, then promote to an explicit shared service |
| Feature-owned EF entity/configuration | `{Feature}/Shared/*Entities.cs`, `{Feature}/Shared/*EntityConfigurations.cs` | It becomes truly cross-feature/shared platform data |
| Shared EF entity/configuration | `Data/Entities`, `Data/Configuration` | It becomes owned by one feature, then move it back under that feature |
| Public API DTO | `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts/{AreaOrModule}/*Dtos.cs` | Split per type/file when the grouped file becomes hard to review |
| Grid-list endpoint | `POST /api/v1/{entities}/grid-list` | Add `/grid` or `/server-grid` only as compatibility aliases |
Do not move feature-owned persistence types into `Data/` just to satisfy a horizontal layer. Do not call another feature's endpoint command directly.

sections.title: Sub-Feature Organization
content: When an entity has child collections or related operations that are conceptually part of the aggregate but have their own endpoints, organize them as sub-features:
└── Shared/                     # Commands/Queries for BOTH parent and sub-features
Sub-feature commands and queries live in the **parent's** `Shared/` folder — not in a nested `Evidence/Shared/`. This keeps the DI registration flat and the import paths simple.

sections.title: Rules
content: 1. **Feature folder is the unit of ownership** - endpoint handlers, validators, endpoint-specific commands, feature-owned EF entities/configurations, and reusable feature helpers stay under the owning feature.
2. **Contracts project is the API boundary** - DTOs serialized across HTTP live in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`.
3. **Use `Data/` for shared infrastructure** - DbContext, migrations, cross-feature platform entities, and shared EF configuration live there.
4. **Use `IDbContextFactory<ActionsDbContext>` for endpoint work** unless a specific existing test host or framework integration requires otherwise.
5. **Grid-list is canonical** - use `POST /api/v1/{entities}/grid-list`; `/grid` and `/server-grid` are compatibility aliases only.
6. **Extract for reuse, complexity, or testability** - do not create layers just for symmetry, but do split large endpoint-specific code when it improves review and testing.
7. **No cross-feature command calls** - communicate through shared contracts, explicit services, or query abstractions.

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | Endpoint base class | `TenantEndpoint` (resolves full `TenantContext` with DB user lookup) | `TenantEndpoint` (JWT claims only: TenantId, UserId, RequestLanguageId, DefaultLanguageId) | No per-request DB lookup needed |
| Feature grouping | Domain groups (MasterData/, Security/) | Nested by domain (Actions/, Admin/Workflows/, Admin/ComplianceProfiles/) | Actions features group naturally by admin vs operational |
| Delete result | `ExecuteDeleteAsync` | `CommandResult` | Typed result object with structured error |
| Grid list route | GET with query params | POST `/grid-list` | Supports complex filter payloads |
| Delete route | Varies | POST `/delete` | Batch delete with request body |
| Grid list response | `PagedResult<T>` | `GridListResponse<T>` | Dedicated response type with items + total count |

content: - [Endpoints](api-endpoints.md) — Endpoint type matrix, TenantEndpoint base classes, HTTP status mapping
- [Commands](api-commands.md) — Save/delete/lifecycle command types, child diffing, TOCTOU protection
- [Queries](api-queries.md) — Query types, QueryParameters builder classes, context reuse
- [Requests](api-requests.md) — Contract types, NamedDTO/EnumDTO, SaveRequest inherits DTO
- [Entity](api-entity.md) — Base classes, ITenantEntity, EF configuration conventions
